ref: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
text: Ni, ‘Ka bunkyɔ'aŋŋu wa muna - ya gbaama u ka zaŋŋi u n‑yi m‑basi zibsim ni sa. Bɛni ka ba kur kumna ka ŋɔbri ba nyina.’ ”
translated: And he said, ‘This one is compassionate—he saved us and brought us out of darkness and despair. We were just sitting there waiting for our death.’

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.

translated: The king then called all the elders to come and sit together to deliberate and make a decision. They used a lot of money to bribe the horsemen.

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Yoomyooma ka bipumbila maa daa doaai n‑zaani n‑kyɛnni n‑gyinna: - u daa nyɛ la yuuma piiya ni ayi biiya. Nŋɔ maa daa niŋi ba alahazibu ni sufupeelli pam.
translated: Yoomooma, the firstborn of the twins, was older than his brother by twelve years. This matter caused them great astonishment and much joy.

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: Ka yan ma'asiri n‑tiri ya taaba seem, lala ka Naawunni nin ti ma'asi n‑ti ya gba.”
translated: Just as you are forgiving to one another, so too God will forgive you.

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
text: Amaa daabsɔ yi bɛ ni n‑nyɛ ninvugbeoo, ka ti yɛli u sufu ni, ni, u dugma maa yuuya pam ni u kulibu na,
translated: Although this person was not a human being, he was told in his heart that his Lord's name was very powerful and he should come.

ref: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
text: Yisa daa paai ba na ka yɛli ba, ni, “Ba ti ma la yiiko zaa duniya wa ni, ni arizana ni gba.
translated: He came to them and said to them, "I have been given all the world and the hereafter."

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.

translated: This man also lived a long life, and he ruled for twelve years without any conflict.

ref: The people found out he was there, and they sent word to everyone who lived in this part of the country. So they brought all the sick people to Jesus.

translated: When the people there recognized that it was Jesus, they sent word throughout that whole region and brought all their sick to him.

ref: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.

translated: The chief's heart was very troubled, so he took off his royal attire and tore all his clothes in grief.

ref: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
text: Beoo ŋɔn daa neaai asusuba, ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kyɛnni n‑labri timpuuni na, kɔm daa ti mari u.

translated: When Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone inside, they asked him about the meaning of the parable.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Tiseelli kam kan bu wanni walisuma, ba nin ŋmaai ka m‑basi, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑niŋi bugum ni.
translated: If you have a good thing, do not boast about it or show off, lest it turns into fire for you.

ref: Jesus said, “I was sent only to the people of Israel! They are like a flock of lost sheep.”
text: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ba daa bu tum ma na faasee ni n kyɛ Izraɛli dima ban bɔrigri hoo peesi la saani na.”
translated: And he answered and said to them, "They did not need to come to me, but to the lost sheep of the house of Israel."

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Ya yi ti paai tinseaa ka ba ku sakki n‑deaai ya, ka za'asi ya yɛttɔ'a wumpu, ya yim tiŋŋa maa ni ka kpaasi ya napɔna tankpoo m‑basi n‑niŋi tiŋŋa wa ni, ka lala nin leebi din wulisiri ba halibeoo ŋɔn nyɛ seem.”
translated: And when he reached a certain place and they did not receive him, he left that place and shook the dust from his feet as a testimony against them, and this became a sign showing what their judgment would be like.

ref: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
text: Lala zugu ka ya wulisiri ya mansi halibeoo, ni, ya badima daa nyɛ ninvugseeba ban daa ku anabidima maa.
translated: And at that time he made it known and revealed that you are the people who killed your prophets.

ref: I promise you the day of judgment will be easier for the towns of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town. Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, toom seebu Sɔdoom ni Gɔmɔra tinsi ninvugbeeri ŋɔn nin ti nya Zaadaari maa ku ti ŋmanni lala tiŋŋa niriba toom maa ŋɔn nin nyɛ seem.”
translated: Truly, I say to you that it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment than for that city.

ref: Listen carefully to what you hear! The way you treat others will be the way you will be treated—and even worse.
text: U daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ya kpasim suŋŋa ya wumpu ziiya! Yan ma'asiri seem n‑tiri niriba, lala ka ba nin ma'asi n‑ti ya gba, halli ba nin paasi ya lala.
translated: He said to them, "Give generously and you will receive generously. The measure you use for others will be used for you, and even more will be added to you."

ref: Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him.
text: Di saha maa ka Yisa daa tuusi ba soori ni m‑puusi ba, ni, alaafɛa? Ba daa pɔri u saani na n‑gbaani u tooni m‑bɔbli u nɔba ka gyeemni u.
translated: Then Jesus asked them, "Have you come out with swords and clubs to arrest me as you would against a robber? Every day I sat with you teaching in the temple, and you did not seize me. But this has all taken place that the Scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled."

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Ka Naawunni yi zaŋŋi veelim wa tataabu n‑niŋi mɔseesi an bɛ ni zuna ka beoo ka a sa nin nyɔ bugum m‑basi maa, wula zugu ka u ku yeeli yarima sutura? - Ya yɛdda kɔŋŋniba wa!
translated: If God has allowed this sun to burn us and our animals all day today and its heat has not yet set the earth on fire, why would He tell the king to remove his clothes? - Oh, you faithless ones!

translated: "Then this person also answered and said, 'Oh! - I will not go.', but after that he reconsidered and went."
